summary: Red review of a possible same-project/source-mixing guard for L64 qv6_001. The guard is worth pursuing, but only as project-identity scoping plus a no-API diagnostic gate. Same issuer is not a safe boundary, and a broad hard same-project rule can create false abstentions or drop legitimate multi-document evidence.

models.review_method: Read-only local artifact/source inspection and proposal writing. No RAG run, no generator call, no judge call, no API/model call.

outputs.description: Contract output for the L65 same-project/source-mixing guard red review.
Human-readable red-review note covering false positives, recall risk, no-API fixtures, and patch placement.

retrieval_metrics.retrieved_orgs_shape: all retrieved orgs are the target issuer

judge_result.reason_shape: The judge accepted the UICC core facts but penalized the answer for adding operation/management/reporting material from same-issuer, different-project Basic Academic Data Center style documents.

observed_failure.why_current_metrics_miss_it: scripts/evaluate.py retrieval coverage only checks target_orgs in retrieved_orgs, so same-issuer contamination can still score coverage=1.0 and mrr=1.0. The deterministic answer diagnostics currently focus on abstention quality, not project-identity purity.

proposal.bottom_line: Patch retrieval/context scoping first, then add an evaluation/report diagnostic. Do not rely on a generation prompt-only fix, and do not treat issuer equality as source safety.

recommended_patch_order.recommendation: After retrieval and before generation, group chunks by project identity using normalized org plus business title and, when available, announcement/file identity. For issuer+fragment queries like qv6_001, select a dominant target project only when query/title evidence is strong, then exclude same-issuer chunks from other projects from the generation context or mark retrieval_scope_mixed_project.
recommended_patch_order.why: The generator in src/generator.py already has source-use instructions, but L64 shows it can blend same-issuer context when irrelevant project chunks are present. Removing or flagging mixed-project context is more deterministic than asking the model to self-police.

recommended_patch_order.recommendation: Add a no-API source-mixing diagnostic that records project_scope_status, project_groups_seen, dominant_project, off_project_chunk_count, and answer_off_project_citation_or_topic_hits. Keep this diagnostic out of EDD until fixture-proven.
recommended_patch_order.why: Judge scores and org coverage missed the regression. A local diagnostic can block report-ready promotion even when relevance and coverage are high.

recommended_patch_order.recommendation: Only after retrieval/context scoping is in place, optionally add a narrow prompt line that says not to combine documents with different business names under the same issuer. Do not make prompt mutation the primary fix.
recommended_patch_order.why: Prompt-only changes are hard to prove without API calls and risk moving the failure rather than preventing it.

rejected_designs.reason: L64 is direct counterevidence: all qv6_001 retrieved_orgs are the target issuer, yet the answer mixed in unrelated same-issuer project material.
Some valid answers require multiple chunks and source types for one project. A hard rule can also false-abstain on noisy fragments before a project is confidently identified.
Removing suspicious lines after generation is brittle because it requires knowing all off-project topics and citations. It should be a report diagnostic or fallback, not the first fix.
The judge caught this one with groundedness=4, but other exposed cases show judge blindness. Source-mixing proof should be deterministic.

false_positive_and_recall_risks.description: A real project can have overview, SFR, operation-support, CSV summary, and attachment chunks with different filenames or sections. The guard must not flag these as mixed-project when business title/announcement identity match.
Mojibake, spacing, punctuation, and partial title variants can make one project look like several. Normalization and fuzzy title matching should be used for grouping, with conservative thresholds.

For ambiguous fragments, selecting the largest same-issuer group can lock onto the wrong project. If query evidence does not support the dominant project, the correct behavior is ambiguity/ask-for-more-info, not forced pruning.
qv6_001 is supposed to recover a project from a partial title plus issuer hint. Overly strict exact-title filters before candidate discovery can drop the target UICC chunks.
General report/project-management clauses may look relevant to operation support but often belong to another same-issuer project. They should be allowed only when project identity matches.
qv6_003 shows a passed answer despite mixed-org retrieval. A source-mixing guard must separate retrieval-scope failure, mixed-context warning, and generation evidence-use failure.
qv6_010 should abstain on a generic fragment with multiple candidate projects. A same-project guard must not silently choose one candidate just because the issuer or title words recur.

no_api_fixture_proof_plan.setup: In-memory chunks share the same issuer and normalized UICC project title but come from overview, SFR, and operation/support sections.
In-memory chunks include target UICC chunks plus same-issuer Basic Academic Data Center chunks containing generic reporting/project-management text.
no_api_fixture_proof_plan.expected: project_scope_status=mixed_project_same_issuer, off_project_chunk_count>0; distractor chunks are excluded before generation or the diagnostic blocks report-ready promotion.

no_api_fixture_proof_plan.setup: qv6_001-like partial title plus issuer hint with a dominant UICC group and weak off-project distractors.
no_api_fixture_proof_plan.expected: dominant_project=UICC, filtered_context uses only UICC project chunks, no false abstention.

no_api_fixture_proof_plan.setup: qv6_010-like generic title words with two or more plausible project groups.
no_api_fixture_proof_plan.expected: project_scope_status=ambiguous_project_identity and no arbitrary context pruning to a single issuer/project.

no_api_fixture_proof_plan.setup: Same project appears with shortened, spaced, punctuation-varied, or mojibake-different titles.
no_api_fixture_proof_plan.expected: Normalization groups them as one project rather than producing a false mixed-project flag.

no_api_fixture_proof_plan.setup: Answer cites document numbers or topics assigned to off-project chunks after mixed same-issuer context is provided.
Retrieved chunks do not include the target project or are mixed without a safe dominant project.
no_api_fixture_proof_plan.expected: Label retrieval_scope_target_missing or retrieval_scope_ambiguous, not generation_source_mixing.

implementation_guidance_if_reopened: Add a small pure helper such as project_identity(metadata) and group_project_scope(chunks, query) so it can be fixture-tested without API calls.
Use retrieved chunks only for runtime decisions. Do not scan global source files to justify a generated answer.
Project identity should be normalized org plus normalized business title, with announcement number or file path used as tie-breakers when present.

Run the context filter before _csv_summary_backfill or ensure backfill also checks the selected project identity. The existing _csv_summary_backfill already binds by single retrieved org and title match, but same-issuer title collisions are the risk to keep explicit.
Return trace fields from RAGPipeline.ask such as project_scope_status, project_groups, selected_project_identity, excluded_chunk_count, and source_mixing_issues.
Extend scripts/evaluate.py diagnostics rather than changing the EDD score immediately. Promote only after fixture results show low false-positive risk.

allowed_summary_language: The guard detects same-issuer, different-project context mixing that org coverage cannot see.
The proposed proof is no-API and fixture-based before any paid rerun.
The recommended fix is project-scoped context assembly plus deterministic diagnostics, not a prompt-only patch.
disallowed_summary_language: hard same-project filtering should apply to every query
judge groundedness alone proves source purity

commands_run: PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json extraction attempt for qv6_001 from L64 details.json
Get-ChildItem -Filter 'l65_same_project_guard_red_review*' in the run worker_outputs directory
observed_results: L64 qv6_001 has coverage=1.0, first_hit_rank=1, all retrieved_orgs equal to the target issuer, judge groundedness=4, relevance=5.
The L64 judge reason identifies extra operation/management/reporting material from same-issuer but different-project documents as the groundedness penalty.
eval/questions_v6_metamorphic_frozen_first_run.json defines qv6_001 as title_fragment_scope_recovery and qv6_010 as the generic-fragment abstention negative control.
src/rag.py retrieves chunks, optionally backfills CSV summary rows, then passes chunks to src/generator.py; this is the natural place to add project-scope tracing or filtering.
src/generator.py already contains instructions and guards against some evidence-use/ambiguous-title failures, showing a prompt-only strategy is insufficient for this source-mixing shape.
scripts/evaluate.py coverage is org-based, so it cannot detect same-issuer different-project mixing by itself.
scripts/check_evidence_guard_fixtures.py demonstrates the desired no-API fixture pattern using in-memory chunks and deterministic guard helpers.

PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json could not parse the L64 details file because the raw artifact appears JSON-like but malformed around embedded text/quotes; the raw file read still provided the needed qv6_001 fields and judge reason.
